title: Secrets, Provider Data Terms, Privacy, Retention, and Sensitive Uses · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent organization supplies a different user population, task distribution, tool set, risk tier, model response, resource budget, accessibility need, and failure condition and transfers secrets, provider data terms, privacy, retention, and sensitive uses into a changed governed secret and agent-data lifecycle. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of governed secret and agent-data lifecycle with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through predi…
objectives: Inject secrets through approved runtime stores, scope and rotate them, prevent prompt or tool exposure, redact derivatives, and destroy test credentials.
Map purpose, legal or organizational authority, fields, sensitivity, users, provider transfer, storage, access, retention, deletion, and aggregation risk.
Record current paid, unpaid, logging, sharing, abuse-monitoring, region, age, and sensitive-use terms and re-review them before release.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Secret Custody
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab agent engineering team handles case 8743 during a multilingual case-routing evaluation. Build deterministic Python evidence to enforce secret custody, data minimization, provider terms, retention, deletion, regional and sensitive-use review, and redacted evidence; last valid checkpoint, audit trail, kill switch, and rollback path must survive failure; then introduce a sensitive action, poisoned memory, leaking log, or computer-use request and deny or pause it. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates.

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Sensitive Use Review
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-sensitive-use-review-13h4c2" that uses case 3916 to enforce secret custody, data minimization, provider terms, retention, deletion, regional and sensitive-use review, and redacted evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Sensitive Use Review.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Sensitive Use Review into observable performance evidence. For Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Sensitive Use Review, keep Sensitive Use Review connected to executable Python model, interaction, tool, policy, state, approval, evaluation, security, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-sensitive-use-review-13h4c2
# Competency: agentpy-sensitive-use-review.
# Case 3916: an accessible public-service intake assistant.
# Operating constraint: sensitive actions require a trustworthy user approval bound to exact arguments.
# Changed case: replace trusted context with untrusted indirect instructions and preserve data-versus-instruction boundaries.
def evidence_agentpy_sensitive_use_review_13h4c2(data_fields: int = 28, approved_fields: int = 12, rejected_fields: int = 16, redacted_logs: int = 28, leaked_secrets: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_13h4c2 = "13h4c2-2c4h31-3916"
    assert evidence_variant_13h4c2
    evidence_axes_13h4c2 = (
        "authority13h4c2",
        "interaction3916",
        "tool2c4h31",
        "state13h4c23916",
        "evaluation391613h4c2",
        "recovery2c4h313916",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_13h4c2)) == 6
    if data_fields < 1 or approved_fields < 0 or rejected_fields < 0:
        return False, data_fields, "privacy-field-count"
    if approved_fields + rejected_fields != data_fields:
        return False, approved_fields, "data-minimization"
    if redacted_logs != data_fields:
        return False, redacted_logs, "log-redaction"
    if leaked_secrets > 0:
        return False, leaked_secrets, "secret-leak"
    assert approved_fields + rejected_fields == data_fields and leaked_secrets == 0
    return True, approved_fields, "governed-agent-data"
